title: General — Multiple-Address Letter Use
plain_language_rule: Use a multiple-address letter when sending identical correspondence with the same subject to two or more addressees or activities within the Department of the Navy. Do not use multiple-address letters when enclosures differ per addressee, addressees are at different command levels, or letters require individual signatures.
source_text: TBD - exact manual text required before activation
renderer_impact: Determines whether the correspondence uses a single-address or multiple-address format, affecting To line, Distribution line, and copy assembly.
notes: This section explains when to use (or not use) multiple-address letters. It is a procedural decision rule, not a layout validation rule. Some sub-conditions (different enclosures per addressee, addressees at different command levels) are judgment calls not amenable to automated enforcement.

title: Listing Addressees — 'To:' Line Only
plain_language_rule: When a multiple-address letter has 4 or fewer addressees, list all addressees in the 'To:' line. Type 'To:' in uppercase followed by a colon, then list each addressee on a separate line. Address addressees as described in Chapter 7.
source_text: TBD - exact manual text required before activation
renderer_impact: For ≤4 addressees, all addressees appear in the 'To:' line. Each addressee on a separate line. No 'Distribution:' line is used.
notes: The ≤4 addressee threshold determines whether addressees appear in the 'To:' line or the 'Distribution:' line. Addressee formatting follows Chapter 7 standards.

title: Listing Addressees — 'Distribution:' Line Only
plain_language_rule: When a multiple-address letter has more than 4 addressees, type 'Distribution:' in uppercase followed by a colon on the second line below the last line of the text. Skip one line after 'Distribution:', then list addressees. Address addressees as described in Chapter 7.
source_text: TBD - exact manual text required before activation
renderer_impact: For >4 addressees, 'Distribution:' appears on the second line below the last line of text, with one blank line before the first addressee. Each addressee on a separate line.
notes: The >4 addressee threshold triggers the 'Distribution:' line format. No 'To:' line is used in this format. Placement relative to the text body and blank-line spacing are layout rules.

title: Listing Addressees — Both 'To:' and 'Distribution:' Line
plain_language_rule: When correspondence is addressed to a group, board, or committee by title, use the 'To:' line for the group title and the 'Distribution:' line for individual members. Type 'To:' in uppercase followed by a colon and the group title. Type 'Distribution:' on the second line below the last line of the text, followed by member names.
source_text: TBD - exact manual text required before activation
renderer_impact: Combines 'To:' and 'Distribution:' lines. 'To:' holds the group/board/committee title. 'Distribution:' is placed on the second line below the last line of text with individual member names below.
notes: This format applies when correspondence is sent to a group, board, or committee addressed by title. The 'To:' line carries the group title; 'Distribution:' lists individual members.

title: Preparing and Signing Copies
plain_language_rule: Use letterhead paper (or plain bond paper if letterhead is not available) for the original only. All copies are photocopies of the signed original. If the originator cannot be reached and copies must be printed separately, include the notation 'ORIGINAL SIGNED BY [name]' on each copy. Copies printed separately must bear the activity's letterhead.
source_text: TBD - exact manual text required before activation
renderer_impact: Determines which pages use letterhead: original only uses letterhead paper; copies are photocopies. If separately printed, copies must include letterhead and 'ORIGINAL SIGNED BY' notation.
notes: This section covers physical paper handling (letterhead paper, photocopying, signing). The 'ORIGINAL SIGNED BY' notation is a text-content requirement for separately printed copies. Most of this section is procedural and not amenable to automated layout validation.

title: Assembly of Multiple-Address Letters
plain_language_rule: Assemble the multiple-address letter in the following order: the original (signature copy or file copy), then the individual address copies. Mail the original to the first addressee on the 'To:' list. For distribution-only letters, assemble the original, file copy, and all distribution copies, then mail the original to the first addressee on the distribution list.
source_text: TBD - exact manual text required before activation
notes: This section covers physical assembly and mailing order procedures. These are mailroom-level processes not amenable to automated validation.
